name: Stir-Fried Scallops in a Basket
recipeIngredient: 12 Fresh scallops (or prawns, Chicken breast, or fish balls)
12 fresh snow peas or thinly sliced broccoli
recipeInstructions:
Contrasting texture, color and appearance are hallmarks of this impressive banquet dish.
Preparation: Slice bamboo shoot across grain into 2-inch triangles. Peel & slice carrots on bias. Remove strings from peas & wash. If substituting broccoli, use peeled stems only; slice thinly on bias. Shred lettuce; arrange like a nest on a round 10-inch serving plate.
Making Potato Basket: Peel, then coarsely shred potatoes. Or, slice potatoes into thin matchsticks. Rinse potatoes in cold water twice; soak them for 30 minutes in cold salted water; rinse & drain. Slowly heat deep-frying oil in wok to medium hot; test potato slice: it should brown in 30 seconds. Dust potatoes with cornstarch so they will stick together when fried. Arrange them in a cross-hatch pattern on a Chinese wire strainer or other suitable strainer to form a deep basket. Press them in position with another strainer. If you use a bowl for pressing, heat it first, because you don't want to cool frying oil. Immerse in oil & deep-fry until potatoes begin to brown. Remove from oil; take away top strainer only; drain.
When ready to serve, heat oil to very hot, but not smoking; deep-fry potatoes in strainer again until they are brown and crisp. Place basket in nest of lettuce.
Blanching: Dip scallops in hot but not boiling water; remove after 15 seconds when scallops are warmed (do not allow them to cook).
Stir-frying: As you heat wok to medium hot, stir-fry ginger slices & garlic to flavor the wok; remove when they start to brown; discard. When wok is hot, add peanut oil & salt; stir. Add bamboo shoots & carrots; stir-fry for 1 minute. Add stock. When stock boils, add scallops, snow peas, sugar, white pepper, gin, and soy sauce. Stir for 1 minute. Add cornstarch paste to thicken. Transfer to potato basket. Serve.
